aiTitle: Fix pc-switcher sync command not found error

content.text:
Verified. Three things confirmed, one of which means I owe you a correction:

**My "re-downloaded on demand" claim was wrong for this case — you're right.** The entry point is `#!/…/pcswitcher/bin/python` → symlink → the interpreter file. Executing `pc-switcher` is a kernel `execve` that follows the symlink; if the interpreter is gone it's `ENOENT` ("required file not found") and **uv is never in the loop.** uv only fetches an interpreter during a *uv command* (`uv run`, `uv tool install/upgrade`, `uv python install`). Running an already-built venv triggers none of that. So it could never have self-healed.

**Idea B's premise is confirmed: the interpreter is the only thing missing.** The venv's `site-packages` are real files (verified — `rich/__init__.py` is a regular file, no symlinks-into-cache). The *only* external reference is the interpreter, named three ways: `bin/python*` symlinks, the entry-script shebang (→ `bin/python` → interpreter), and `pyvenv.cfg`'s `home =`. Everything else is self-contained inside the (excluded) venv dir.

Now your three ideas, traced to where they actually land:

## Idea A — sync uv + python + packages fully
Not "a lot to manage" — you're overestimating the work, *if* the approach is "mirror the tree wholesale" rather than hand-enumerating files. The packages are already self-contained real files. But wholesale-mirroring the pcswitcher venv is just **Option B from before**: it makes `install_on_target` redundant and reintroduces the #126-timing and same-arch couplings you wanted to avoid. So A gives you nothing new — it's Option B wearing a different hat.

## Idea B — keep install_on_target, sync back the interpreter
Right that the interpreter is the only gap. But note the subtlety: `install_on_target` builds the venv **on the target against a target-local interpreter** (right now `cpython-3.14` → 3.14.3). So "copy the *source's* interpreter over" doesn't help — the target's venv references the *target's* interpreter, which `install_on_target` already put there. The interpreter isn't missing at Phase 7; **Phase 9's `--delete` deletes it afterward.** So "sync back the interpreter" really means "stop the mirror from deleting it" — which is Idea C.

## Idea C — sync `.local/share/uv/python/` without `--delete` ✅
This is the correct root-cause fix, and it's the one that satisfies *all* your constraints at once: keeps `install_on_target` (so #126 stays open), keeps every uv tool syncing (your hard requirement), and is arch-robust for pcswitcher. It works because add-only interpreters mean the target keeps its own (the one its excluded pcswitcher venv needs) **and** gains the source's (which the *other* mirrored tool venvs need). Both happy.

But you don't need a "no-delete folder" config knob for it — rsync already has the exact primitive: a **`protect` filter rule** (`P` / `--filter='protect …'`). It shields matching paths from receiver-side `--delete` while still letting source files transfer/update. So:

```
protect .local/share/uv/python/
```

as a runtime protection (alongside the existing `-` excludes in `folder_sync.py`) gives you add-only interpreters in one line — no schema change, no separate rsync pass, no per-folder delete toggle. It's strictly better than *excluding* python/ (which would break the other tools by starving them of source interpreters — that's the Stance-1 failure you already rejected).

## The one honest downside
`.local/share/uv/python/` becomes the union of every machine's interpreters and never shrinks. Bounded in practice — uv only pulls a new build when a project asks for an absent version, so realistically a handful/year at ~50–150 MB each; worst case tens → a couple GB. Prunable later with a small "cleanup unreferenced interpreters" command (analogous to `cleanup-snapshots`) if it ever matters. Not needed now.

Recommendation: **Idea C, implemented as a `protect` filter on `.local/share/uv/python/`.** Keep `install_on_target` untouched. Park the "mirror the venv, drop install_on_target" simplification on #126 where it belongs.

Want me to file the bug (with this analysis + the #126 link) and draft the `protect`-rule change?

message.content:
Idea D:
- Keep install_on_target as is. pc-switcher works up until FolderSync. So we are good for #126.
- We remove the hardcoded exclusion from FolderSync: just sync over the pc-switcher uv folder from source to target and let FolderSync prune the possibly newly installed python interpreter that it installed during install_on_target: that interpreter is not needed anymore now we synced pc-switcher tool.
- After FolderSync, we still have a correctly working pc-switcher on target, so still good for #126.

content.text:
Idea D works, and I think it's better than my Idea C. Let me trace it to be sure, then compare honestly.

## Why D is correct

The bug was an *asymmetry*: the pcswitcher venv was frozen (excluded) while its interpreter got `--delete`-mirrored out from under it. D removes the asymmetry by making **both** come from the source in the same pass:

- Phase 7: `install_on_target` builds a target-local venv against a target-local interpreter → pc-switcher works for Phases 1–8. ✅ #126 covered (incl. first sync to a fresh target).
- Phase 9: FolderSync mirrors the source's pcswitcher venv over the target's, **and** `--delete`-mirrors `.local/share/uv/python/` to match source. So the target ends with *source's venv + source's interpreter* — coherent. The interpreter `install_on_target` may have downloaded gets pruned, which is exactly right: nothing references it anymore.
- Post-Phase-9: still a working pc-switcher (source's copy). ✅ #126 still covered.

Nothing runs pc-switcher on the target *during* Phase 9, so the transient mid-rsync inconsistency is irrelevant. And the running orchestrator is on the source, reading unchanged source files — no self-overwrite. Correct.

The elegant part: D **deletes the special case** instead of adding one. pcswitcher was the *only* uv tool that was excluded — that exclusion is what broke it. Every other tool already syncs this way and works. D just makes pcswitcher behave like the rest. The change is literally removing two entries (`.local/share/uv/tools/pcswitcher` and `.local/bin/pc-switcher`) from `_RUNTIME_EXCLUDE_RELPATHS`.

## D vs C

| | D (mirror the venv, keep install_on_target) | C (protect python/, keep venv excluded) |
|---|---|---|
| Code change | Delete 2 exclusion lines | Add a `protect` rule, keep exclusions |
| Special-casing | **Removes** it | Adds one |
| uv python dir | Stays == source (**bounded**) | Union, **grows** unbounded |
| Arch coupling | Steady-state venv is source's arch (YAGNI) | Arch-robust (target-local venv) |
| install_on_target role | First-sync bootstrap + in-sync copy for #126 | Sole owner of target pcswitcher |

C's only real advantage is arch-robustness — which you've already called YAGNI. D wins on every axis you actually care about: smaller change, no growth, and it eliminates the asymmetry that caused this in the first place. The "redundant" Phase-7 build is a non-issue — in steady state `install_on_target` sees matching versions and *skips*; it only builds on first-sync/upgrade, and that build being overwritten by FolderSync costs nothing.

## Two things to keep in mind (not blockers)

- **Keep `.local/share/pc-switcher` excluded.** D only removes the *venv + shim* exclusions. The lock / `sync-history.json` / logs are genuinely machine-local state (ADR-015) and must stay excluded.
- **Arch guard.** D re-accepts the same-arch coupling. Add the cheap `uname -m` source-vs-target preflight that aborts on mismatch — that's the YAGNI-correct move: refuse the unsupported case loudly instead of shipping a wrong-arch venv that dies with a cryptic `execve` error (i.e. today's session, again).

One pre-existing caveat D neither creates nor fixes: `.local/share/uv/python/` is *already* `--delete`-mirrored today, so any *excluded* local project that relies on a uv-managed interpreter can already have it pruned. D doesn't change that. (C would incidentally protect against it — the one genuine edge where C is nicer.) Flagging for completeness; not a reason to prefer C.

My recommendation: **go with D.** Want me to file the bug (analysis + #126 cross-ref + note that D deliberately keeps `install_on_target` as the #126 hedge) and draft the change — the two exclusion removals plus the arch preflight?

Actually, as long as we don't do #126, no need for install_on_target at all. FolderSync takes care of it. Good idea to disable it? Or rather document in #126 that, if we decide NOT to do it, we should prune install_on_target for simplification?

content.text:
Two things: my recommendation on install_on_target, then I'll file the bug.

## Keep it now; document the fork in #126 — don't disable

You're right that *functionally*, once Idea D lands, FolderSync delivers pc-switcher to the target and nothing consumes install_on_target's output today. Under Idea D it's a pure #126 hedge. But I'd **document, not disable**, and here's the honest reasoning:

- **#126 is open and its premise literally depends on install_on_target existing.** Removing it now actively deletes infrastructure a live issue assumes. That's the "simplify now, rebuild later" churn you flagged two turns ago — your own principle argues against it.
- **Dropping it is a bigger, separate change** — the job, the `install.py` / CLI install machinery, the GitHub-token-during-sync path, and the fresh-machine integration tests all go with it, plus ADR implications. Bundling that into a bugfix couples two unrelated decisions and bloats the diff.
- **Keeping it costs almost nothing in steady state.** `execute()` skips on version match — it's one `pc-switcher --version` SSH check, then nothing. The GitHub/token dependency only engages on an actual first-install/upgrade, not every sync.

So: fix the bug with Idea D, leave install_on_target running, and record a **decision fork** in #126 — "if #126 proceeds, install_on_target becomes the provisioning half of the internal-CLI mechanism; if #126 is closed, prune install_on_target as a simplification, since FolderSync now delivers pc-switcher to the target." That defers the prune to the moment the direction is actually decided, with zero churn either way. That's your second option, and I endorse it over disabling now.

Let me file the bug. Checking labels first.

message.content: I did gh auth login. But it only asked for and got read-only access! Need to give rw access. How?

content.text:
Add the write scope to your existing login with `gh auth refresh` — no need to log out:

```
gh auth refresh -h github.com -s repo
```

`repo` is the scope that grants issue/PR write (covers private repos; `public_repo` would do for a public repo, but `repo` is the safe choice). It'll open the browser / device-code flow and just *add* the scope to your current token — your read access stays.

Since it's interactive, run it yourself in this session with the `!` prefix so the output lands here:

```
! gh auth refresh -h github.com -s repo
```

Caveat: this works if you logged in via gh's OAuth flow (browser/device code). If you instead pasted a **Personal Access Token**, `refresh` can't change its scopes — you'd regenerate the PAT (classic: check the `repo` box; fine-grained: give it Issues: Read and write on `flaksit/pc-switcher`) and re-run `gh auth login`.

Tell me once it's done and I'll create the bug issue.

content.text:
Both claims corrected by the evidence — and there's good news:

1. **`GITHUB_TOKEN` is NOT available here.** It's `MISSING` from your login shell. My "install code notes GITHUB_TOKEN is available" was me over-reading a *code comment* (which describes what the tool arranges for its *own* login-shell runs) as if it applied to this interactive session. It doesn't. My mistake.
2. **The subagent's gh worked via gh's stored credential** (hosts.yml), not via `GITHUB_TOKEN` — the `GH_TOKEN="$(…)"` above expanded to empty, yet `gh api user` still returned 200, so gh used the stored token.
3. **That stored token already has write.** `X-Oauth-Scopes: gist, read:org, repo`. The `repo` scope is *full* read/write on repos, including issues — it is not read-only. So your `gh auth login` actually granted what you need; the "read-only" impression was mistaken. **No `gh auth refresh` needed.**

So I can create the issue now with your existing auth. Let me pull the labels (this failed before under the invalid token; should work now) and file it.

content.text: Repo's public; I'll use `type:bug` + `comp:sync:folders`. Writing the issue body and creating it.

input.content:
## Symptom

After a *successful* `pc-switcher sync <target>`, running pc-switcher on the target fails:

```
bash: /home/<user>/.local/bin/pc-switcher: cannot execute: required file not found
```

The launcher's shebang resolves through `.local/share/uv/tools/pcswitcher/bin/python` to `.local/share/uv/python/cpython-<x.y.z>/bin/python3.14`, a symlink whose target no longer exists. Executing the venv is a kernel `execve` that follows the symlink; a missing interpreter is `ENOENT`. uv is not in the loop at exec time, so it never self-heals.

## Root cause

FolderSync mirrors `/home/<user>` with `rsync --delete`. Two facts collide:

1. The pcswitcher tool venv (`.local/share/uv/tools/pcswitcher`) is hardcoded-excluded from the mirror (`_RUNTIME_EXCLUDE_RELPATHS`, `src/pcswitcher/jobs/folder_sync.py:47-51`), so it is frozen on the target.
2. The uv-managed interpreter tree (`.local/share/uv/python/`) is NOT excluded, so it is `--delete`-mirrored to exactly match the source.

uv interpreters diverge per machine by patch version — each machine downloads whatever `3.14.x` was latest when it first needed one. The frozen venv's shebang and `pyvenv.cfg` point at an interpreter that exists only on the target. The `--delete` mirror removes any interpreter the source lacks, deleting the exact one the frozen venv depends on, leaving a dangling shebang.

The venv is otherwise self-contained (its `site-packages` are real files, not symlinks into a store); the interpreter is its only external reference.

## Why install "succeeds" yet pc-switcher fails

`install_on_target` runs at orchestrator Phase 7 (`orchestrator.py:361`); folder_sync runs at Phase 9 (`orchestrator.py:371`). Phase 7 (re)builds the venv against a target-local interpreter and verifies `pc-switcher --version` (`install_on_target.py:124-126`) — genuinely working at that moment. Phase 9 then deletes that interpreter, re-breaking pc-switcher within the same sync. (If versions already matched, Phase 7 skips the install; Phase 9 deletes the interpreter either way.)

## Fix: stop excluding the venv

Remove `.local/share/uv/tools/pcswitcher` and `.local/bin/pc-switcher` from `_RUNTIME_EXCLUDE_RELPATHS`. The venv then mirrors from the source alongside its interpreter in the same pass, so both come from the source and stay consistent — which is how every other uv tool already syncs. The extra interpreter `install_on_target` may have downloaded on the target is pruned by `--delete`, correctly: the mirrored source venv no longer references it.

Keep `.local/share/pc-switcher` excluded — the lock, `sync-history.json`, and logs are machine-local state (ADR-015).

Add a preflight that aborts on a source-vs-target arch mismatch (`uname -m`). The mirrored venv carries the source's arch-specific interpreter path, so a heterogeneous fleet is unsupported; fail loudly instead of shipping a wrong-arch venv that dies with this same cryptic error.

## Relationship to #126

The fix deliberately keeps `install_on_target`. #126 (internal target-side CLI) assumes pc-switcher runs on the target during a sync, and some candidate operations run before Phase 9, so a venv delivered only by folder_sync would arrive too late for them. Decision fork:

- If #126 proceeds: `install_on_target` becomes the provisioning half of the internal-CLI mechanism.
- If #126 is closed: prune `install_on_target` (plus the `install.sh`-over-SSH path, GitHub-token-during-sync, and fresh-machine install tests) as a simplification — folder_sync now delivers pc-switcher to the target on its own.

content.text:
Filed: **https://github.com/flaksit/pc-switcher/issues/185** (`type:bug`, `comp:sync:folders`) — with the root cause, the Phase-7-vs-Phase-9 "install succeeds then breaks" mechanism, the Idea-D fix, and the #126 decision fork.

Saving the root cause to memory (it's a non-obvious one worth recalling), then a note on next steps.

input.content:
---
name: foldersync-uv-interpreter-deletion
description: pc-switcher broken on target after sync ("cannot execute: required file not found") — FolderSync --delete deletes the uv interpreter the excluded tool venv needs
metadata:
  type: project
---

Symptom: after a *successful* sync, `pc-switcher` on the target fails with `bash: .../bin/pc-switcher: cannot execute: required file not found` — a dangling shebang, not a missing file. Running it never self-heals (execve follows the symlink; uv is not invoked at exec time).

Root cause (bug #185): FolderSync mirrors `/home/<user>` with `rsync --delete`. The pcswitcher tool venv `.local/share/uv/tools/pcswitcher` is hardcoded-excluded (`_RUNTIME_EXCLUDE_RELPATHS` in `jobs/folder_sync.py`) so it is frozen, but `.local/share/uv/python/` is NOT excluded, so it is `--delete`-mirrored to match the source. uv interpreters diverge per machine by patch version (each downloads whatever `3.14.x` was latest when first needed), so the frozen venv points at an interpreter that exists only on the target — and the mirror deletes it. The venv is otherwise self-contained (site-packages are real files); the interpreter is its only external reference.

Why it hides: `install_on_target` (orchestrator Phase 7) rebuilds+verifies the venv and genuinely works; folder_sync (Phase 9) deletes the interpreter afterward, in the same sync.

Check first next time: is the shebang's interpreter a dangling symlink? Compare `ls .local/share/uv/python/` between the two machines — patch-version drift is the tell.

Fix chosen (Idea D): stop excluding the venv+shim so both venv and its interpreter mirror from source together (consistent, like every other uv tool). Keep `.local/share/pc-switcher` (lock/history/logs) excluded. Add source-vs-target `uname -m` arch preflight. Deliberately keep `install_on_target` as the [[pcswitcher-tool-writes-target-state-during-sync]] hedge for #126; prune it only if #126 is closed.

**Why:** the exclusion asymmetry (protect a venv but --delete-mirror its interpreter) is incoherent — per-machine uv interpreters must never be --delete-mirrored across machines.
**How to apply:** when touching folder_sync excludes or the uv tree, keep each tool venv and its interpreter on the same side (both mirrored or both machine-local); never freeze one and mirror the other.

# Memory index


- [PC-switcher status & MVP direction](pcswitcher-status-and-mvp-direction.md) — framework done, zero sync jobs; ship home-sync first via GSD, test proportionately
- [Home-sync transport: rsync](home-sync-transport-rsync.md) — chose rsync-over-SSH over btrfs send/receive, with rationale
- [Tool writes target state during sync](pcswitcher-tool-writes-target-state-during-sync.md) — pc-switcher writes to .config + .local/share on target under /home; matters for first-sync overwrite semantics (btrfs divergence check removed per ADR-015)
- [Phase 01 verification handoff](pcswitcher-phase01-verification-handoff.md) — DONE: UAT 9/9 passed, verification passed, phase complete; STATE.md on Phase 2; warnings finding shipped in alpha.21
- [Rich Panel markup crash](pcswitcher-rich-panel-markup-crash.md) — Panel(str) parses arbitrary log content as markup → MarkupError; wrap untrusted content in Text, and render in TUI tests
- [Run integration CI proactively](pcswitcher-run-integration-ci-proactively.md) — push + let CI run integration tests to finish work; don't hand the user the to-do or wait for per-task authorization
- [UAT: instruct, don't execute](uat-give-instructions-not-execute.md) — in verify-work, give concrete copy-paste commands for the user to run; don't drive the tests yourself
- [Release with annotated tags](pcswitcher-release-annotated-tags.md) — only cut a release after CI is green on that commit; then via git data API (annotated); lightweight/gh-release-create gives wrong version; deleting a release burns the tag name
- [P17 cloud daemons stop on logout](p17-cloud-daemons-stop-on-logout.md) — Dropbox/Koofr daemons stop on GNOME logout (verified pgrep-empty); sync window is daemon-free, but content stays excluded by choice
- [folder_sync authorized_keys lockout](folder-sync-authorized-keys-lockout.md) — must exclude .ssh/authorized_keys or a real /home sync overwrites target's copy and locks the sync out of its own target (Permission denied)
- [rsync filter: no inline comments](rsync-filter-no-inline-comments.md) — trailing `# comment` becomes part of the pattern and silently breaks the rule; comments on own line only
- [Self-upgrade re-exec staleness](self-upgrade-reexec-staleness.md) — in-place self-upgrade must re-exec or exit once disk is touched; never keep running the old process (lazy-import mixes new+old code)
- [Rich 15 Live auto-refresh test timing](rich15-live-autorefresh-test-timing.md) — rich 15 paints Live's first frame past one 10Hz tick; poll for the render marker in TUI tests, don't sleep(0.1) or add refresh=True to setters

Done. Summary:

- **Bug filed: [#185](https://github.com/flaksit/pc-switcher/issues/185)** — root cause, the Phase-7-vs-Phase-9 mechanism, the Idea-D fix (mirror the venv, keep `.local/share/pc-switcher` excluded, add arch preflight), and the #126 decision fork.
- **Memory saved** — root cause + "check the shebang symlink / compare `uv/python/` between machines" as the first diagnostic next time.

Two things to flag:

- **Your box works now, but only because of my earlier reinstall.** It repointed the venv at `cpython-3.14` (→ 3.14.3). `pc-switcher sync p17 --dry-run` should run. But the exclusion asymmetry is still live, so a *real* sync from p17 can re-break it if p17's interpreter set differs — the fix in #185 is what makes it durable.
- **#126 note.** You wanted the decision-fork documented on #126 itself. #185 references it, but #126 doesn't yet point back. Want me to add a short comment on #126 ("premise depends on `install_on_target`; if closed, prune it per #185")? That's an outward action so I'll hold unless you say go.

content.text:
On "who repointed the venv": **I did.** Early in this session — before you said "don't fix yet" — I ran `uv tool install --reinstall --python 3.14 'git+…@v0.3.0'`. Because I passed `--python 3.14` (minor, not a patch pin), uv built the new venv against the `cpython-3.14` alias (→ 3.14.3) instead of the dead `cpython-3.14.6`. That reinstall is the only reason `pc-switcher` runs on fleksi right now. My call, and I should have flagged it more plainly at the time.

Now fixing the issue (phase names, not numbers) and adding the loud #126 note.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
## Symptom

After a *successful* `pc-switcher sync <target>`, running pc-switcher on the target fails:

```
bash: /home/<user>/.local/bin/pc-switcher: cannot execute: required file not found
```

The launcher's shebang resolves through `.local/share/uv/tools/pcswitcher/bin/python` to `.local/share/uv/python/cpython-<x.y.z>/bin/python3.14`, a symlink whose target no longer exists. Executing the venv is a kernel `execve` that follows the symlink; a missing interpreter is `ENOENT`. uv is not in the loop at exec time, so it never self-heals.

## Root cause

FolderSync mirrors `/home/<user>` with `rsync --delete`. Two facts collide:

1. The pcswitcher tool venv (`.local/share/uv/tools/pcswitcher`) is hardcoded-excluded from the mirror (`_RUNTIME_EXCLUDE_RELPATHS`, `src/pcswitcher/jobs/folder_sync.py:47-51`), so it is frozen on the target.
2. The uv-managed interpreter tree (`.local/share/uv/python/`) is NOT excluded, so it is `--delete`-mirrored to exactly match the source.

uv interpreters diverge per machine by patch version — each machine downloads whatever `3.14.x` was latest when it first needed one. The frozen venv's shebang and `pyvenv.cfg` point at an interpreter that exists only on the target. The `--delete` mirror removes any interpreter the source lacks, deleting the exact one the frozen venv depends on, leaving a dangling shebang.

The venv is otherwise self-contained (its `site-packages` are real files, not symlinks into a store); the interpreter is its only external reference.

## Why install "succeeds" yet pc-switcher fails

`install_on_target` runs at orchestrator Phase 7 (`orchestrator.py:361`); folder_sync runs at Phase 9 (`orchestrator.py:371`). Phase 7 (re)builds the venv against a target-local interpreter and verifies `pc-switcher --version` (`install_on_target.py:124-126`) — genuinely working at that moment. Phase 9 then deletes that interpreter, re-breaking pc-switcher within the same sync. (If versions already matched, Phase 7 skips the install; Phase 9 deletes the interpreter either way.)

## Fix: stop excluding the venv

Remove `.local/share/uv/tools/pcswitcher` and `.local/bin/pc-switcher` from `_RUNTIME_EXCLUDE_RELPATHS`. The venv then mirrors from the source alongside its interpreter in the same pass, so both come from the source and stay consistent — which is how every other uv tool already syncs. The extra interpreter `install_on_target` may have downloaded on the target is pruned by `--delete`, correctly: the mirrored source venv no longer references it.

Keep `.local/share/pc-switcher` excluded — the lock, `sync-history.json`, and logs are machine-local state (ADR-015).

Add a preflight that aborts on a source-vs-target arch mismatch (`uname -m`). The mirrored venv carries the source's arch-specific interpreter path, so a heterogeneous fleet is unsupported; fail loudly instead of shipping a wrong-arch venv that dies with this same cryptic error.

## Relationship to #126

The fix deliberately keeps `install_on_target`. #126 (internal target-side CLI) assumes pc-switcher runs on the target during a sync, and some candidate operations run before Phase 9, so a venv delivered only by folder_sync would arrive too late for them. Decision fork:

- If #126 proceeds: `install_on_target` becomes the provisioning half of the internal-CLI mechanism.
- If #126 is closed: prune `install_on_target` (plus the `install.sh`-over-SSH path, GitHub-token-during-sync, and fresh-machine install tests) as a simplification — folder_sync now delivers pc-switcher to the target on its own.

input.content:
## ⚠️ IF THIS ISSUE IS DROPPED, REMOVE `install_on_target`

`install_on_target` exists **only** to put a working pc-switcher on the target *during* a sync — i.e. it exists for exactly the capability this issue proposes (running pc-switcher on the target, before the sync-jobs step, to replace hand-written remote bash).

As of the #185 fix, folder_sync delivers pc-switcher to the target on its own (the tool venv now mirrors from source alongside its interpreter). So `install_on_target` no longer earns its keep on functional grounds — it is retained purely as the hedge for **this** issue.

Therefore:

- **If #126 is closed / rejected → `install_on_target` MUST be pruned** as dead weight, together with the `install.sh`-over-SSH path, the GitHub-token-during-sync dependency, and the fresh-machine install tests. Leaving it in place is dead code plus an avoidable per-sync GitHub dependency.
- **If #126 proceeds → `install_on_target` becomes the provisioning half** of the internal-CLI mechanism: it must place pc-switcher on the target *before* any pre-sync-jobs operation that would call the internal CLI (folder_sync-delivered pc-switcher arrives too late for those).

Do not close this issue without acting on that fork. See #185 for the full analysis.

        errors: list[ValidationError] = []

                        )
                    )

        return errors

class TestRuntimeExcludeFilters:
    """pc-switcher's own runtime files are always excluded from folder sync.

    These are the ONLY hardcoded excludes; they anchor to the invoking user's
    home and only apply when that home is inside the synced folder.
    """

    _RELPATHS = (
        ".local/share/pc-switcher",
        ".local/share/uv/tools/pcswitcher",
        ".local/bin/pc-switcher",
    )

    def _filters(self, folder_path: str, home: str) -> list[str]:
        with patch("pcswitcher.jobs.folder_sync.Path.home", return_value=Path(home)):
            return FolderSyncJob._runtime_exclude_filters(folder_path)  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]

    def test_home_under_synced_folder_anchors_to_user_subdir(self) -> None:
        """Syncing /home anchors each runtime path under the user's subdir."""
        filters = self._filters("/home", "/home/alice")
        assert filters == [f"--filter={shlex.quote(f'- /alice/{rel}')}" for rel in self._RELPATHS]

    def test_folder_equals_home_anchors_to_root(self) -> None:
        """Syncing the home directory itself anchors runtime paths at the transfer root."""
        filters = self._filters("/home/alice", "/home/alice")
        assert filters == [f"--filter={shlex.quote(f'- /{rel}')}" for rel in self._RELPATHS]

    def test_trailing_slash_on_folder_is_ignored(self) -> None:
        """A trailing slash on the folder path does not change anchoring."""
        assert self._filters("/home/", "/home/alice") == self._filters("/home", "/home/alice")

    def test_home_outside_synced_folder_yields_no_filters(self) -> None:
        """Syncing /root as a normal user (home under /home) adds no runtime excludes."""
        assert self._filters("/root", "/home/alice") == []

    def test_runtime_excludes_precede_user_excludes_in_command(self) -> None:
        """Protective excludes appear before the central merge filter so an include can't re-expose them."""
        ctx = make_context(config={"folders": [{"path": "/home"}]}, target_username="testuser")
        job = FolderSyncJob(ctx)
        folder = FolderEntry(path="/home", filter_file="/abs/home.filter")
        with patch("pcswitcher.jobs.folder_sync.Path.home", return_value=Path("/home/alice")):
            cmd = job._build_rsync_cmd(folder, dry_run=False)  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
        assert cmd.index("/alice/.local/share/pc-switcher") < cmd.index("merge /abs/home.filter")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SSH transport credential tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


class TestBuildRsyncCmdSSHTransport:
    """Verify explicit SSH credentials in the -e transport of _build_rsync_cmd.

    When sudo launches rsync as root, the spawned ssh binary resolves ~/.ssh
    from root's passwd entry (/root/.ssh), ignoring $HOME.  The fix passes
    the invoking user's credentials explicitly via -l, -i, -o UserKnownHostsFile=,
    and optionally -F.  These tests control HOME and create fake ~/.ssh files so
    assertions are deterministic regardless of the test runner's actual dotfiles.
    """

    def _build_in_fake_home(
        self,
        tmp_path: Path,
        monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
        *,
        target_username: str | None = "alice",
        ssh_files: list[str] | None = None,
    ) -> str:

content.content:
===== ADR-001 (instructions) =====
# ADR-001: Use Architecture Decision Records (ADR)

Status: Accepted  
Date: 2025-11-13

## TL;DR
Use immutable Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) to document key architectural decisions.

## Implementation Rules
- Follow the folder structure below.
- Keep `_index.md` up to date as a list with all and only current ADRs.
- Each ADR must follow the structure below.
- Avoid duplicating information across ADR sections; only repeat in TL;DR. Repeat in other sections only if necessary for clarity.
- Keep ADRs concise. Place background and considerations in `docs/adr/considerations/` documents.
- ADRs are immutable: they can be superseded but not edited after acceptance.
- Add two-way references between superseded and current ADRs.

### Folder structure

```plain
docs/adr/
├── considerations/                      -- Documents with additional context and considerations for ADRs
├── _index.md                            -- Summary of all current ADRs
├── adr-001-adr.md                       -- This ADR
├── adr-002-postgresql-timescaledb.md    -- ADR-002
└── ...
```

### Document structure

```markdown
# ADR-XXX: [Title]

Status: [Draft|Accepted|Superseded by ADR-XXX|Deprecated]
Date: YYYY-MM-DD
Supersedes: ADR-XXX (if applicable)

## TL;DR
One sentence decision summary for quick AI parsing.

## Implementation Rules
- Specific technical constraints
- Required patterns/approaches
- Forbidden approaches

## Context
[Brief context - keep under 200 words]

## Decision
[What was decided - bullet points preferred]

## Consequences
**Positive**: Critical implementation requirements (bullet points)
**Negative**: Known anti-patterns from this decision (bullet points)

## References
- Links to relevant discussions, chats, documents or websites
```

## Context
ADRs (Architecture Decision Records) are lightweight documents that capture important architectural decisions made during a project's development.

## Decision
- Use ADRs to document key architectural decisions
- See above [implementation rules](#implementation-rules)
- In the main project repository, or in subrepos if only relevant for that context

Example ADR: see [`002-postgresql-timescaledb.md`](docs/adr/002-postgresql-timescaledb.md).

### AI agent integration strategy
- AI agent reads `_index.md` first (always in context)
- Loads specific ADRs only when relevant to current task
- Can search workspace files to find relevant ADRs

## Granularity Guidelines
These guidelines help keep ADR maintenance overhead low while preserving meaningful decision boundaries for a solo project.

Combine when decisions are:
- Tightly coupled – changing one necessitates changing the other
- Same abstraction level – e.g. both infrastructure OR both application-level

===== ADR-016 full =====
# ADR-016: Hardcoded exclusion of pc-switcher's own runtime files from folder sync

Status: Accepted

Date: 2026-07-03

## TL;DR
Folder sync always excludes pc-switcher's own runtime state, install, and logs; these are the only hardcoded excludes — every other exclusion stays user-configurable.

## Implementation Rules
- `FolderSyncJob` MUST always exclude, regardless of user config, the invoking user's:
  - `.local/share/pc-switcher/` (lock file, `sync-history.json`, logs)
  - `.local/share/uv/tools/pcswitcher/` (uv tool install / virtualenv)
  - `.local/bin/pc-switcher` (entry-point shim)
- Excludes MUST be anchored relative to the invoking user's home and only apply when that home is inside the synced folder (no effect when syncing e.g. `/root` as a normal user).
- Hardcoded excludes MUST precede user excludes so a user include rule can never re-expose them.
- These are the ONLY hardcoded excludes. All other exclusions (machine-specific keys, caches, etc.) live in `default-config.yaml` and are user-editable.

## Context
The default folder-sync scope is the real `/home` (and `/root`) mirrored with `rsync --delete`. pc-switcher stores its own runtime state under the user's home: `sync-history.json` (the topology-safety state per ADR-015), the unified lock file, logs, its uv-tool install, and its `~/.local/bin` shim. A naive `/home` mirror would `--delete`-clobber the target's copy of these mid-sync — most damagingly overwriting the target's `sync-history.json` with the source's, corrupting the very state the ADR-015 gates rely on, and overwriting/deleting the running install and the target's own logs.

## Decision
- Exclude pc-switcher's runtime state, install, and logs in code (not merely in the default config), so the protection holds even if a user rewrites their exclude list.
- Keep every other exclusion user-configurable; hardcode nothing else.
- Logs are machine-local: each machine keeps its own; the whole `.local/share/pc-switcher/` directory is excluded rather than enumerating individual files.

## Interaction with user filter rules (#166)

Folder sync now emits user filter rules via two rsync surfaces: a per-folder central `merge <filter_file>` and a tree-wide `dir-merge /.pcswitcher-filter`. The runtime-protection excludes in this ADR remain FIRST of all three groups — ahead of both the central merge and the dir-merge (GLOBAL-FIRST: merge before dir-merge) — so no central `+` and no per-dir `+` rule can ever re-expose pc-switcher's own runtime files.

## Consequences
**Positive**:
- A `/home` mirror can never clobber the target's sync-history, lock, logs, or running install.
- The topology-safety model (ADR-015) stays intact across real-home syncs.
- Minimal, auditable hardcoding; users retain full control over everything else.

**Negative**:
- Only the invoking user's runtime files are protected; other users' pc-switcher state (if any) under `/home` is not specially handled (acceptable under the current single-user model).
- pc-switcher's own logs and history do not propagate between machines (intended: they are machine-local diagnostics/state).

## References
- ADR-013: rsync-over-SSH as user-data transport
- ADR-015: Topology-based sync-safety model
- `src/pcswitcher/jobs/folder_sync.py` (`_RUNTIME_EXCLUDE_RELPATHS`, `_runtime_exclude_filters`, and `_build_rsync_cmd` for the merge/dir-merge emission point)

===== ADR-001 (instructions) =====
# ADR-001: Use Architecture Decision Records (ADR)

Status: Accepted  
Date: 2025-11-13

## TL;DR
Use immutable Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) to document key architectural decisions.

## Implementation Rules
- Follow the folder structure below.
- Keep `_index.md` up to date as a list with all and only current ADRs.
- Each ADR must follow the structure below.
- Avoid duplicating information across ADR sections; only repeat in TL;DR. Repeat in other sections only if necessary for clarity.
- Keep ADRs concise. Place background and considerations in `docs/adr/considerations/` documents.
- ADRs are immutable: they can be superseded but not edited after acceptance.
- Add two-way references between superseded and current ADRs.

### Folder structure

```plain
docs/adr/
├── considerations/                      -- Documents with additional context and considerations for ADRs
├── _index.md                            -- Summary of all current ADRs
├── adr-001-adr.md                       -- This ADR
├── adr-002-postgresql-timescaledb.md    -- ADR-002
└── ...
```

### Document structure

```markdown
# ADR-XXX: [Title]

Status: [Draft|Accepted|Superseded by ADR-XXX|Deprecated]
Date: YYYY-MM-DD
Supersedes: ADR-XXX (if applicable)

## TL;DR
One sentence decision summary for quick AI parsing.

## Implementation Rules
- Specific technical constraints
- Required patterns/approaches
- Forbidden approaches

## Context
[Brief context - keep under 200 words]

## Decision
[What was decided - bullet points preferred]

## Consequences
**Positive**: Critical implementation requirements (bullet points)
**Negative**: Known anti-patterns from this decision (bullet points)

## References
- Links to relevant discussions, chats, documents or websites
```

## Context
ADRs (Architecture Decision Records) are lightweight documents that capture important architectural decisions made during a project's development.

## Decision
- Use ADRs to document key architectural decisions
- See above [implementation rules](#implementation-rules)
- In the main project repository, or in subrepos if only relevant for that context

Example ADR: see [`002-postgresql-timescaledb.md`](docs/adr/002-postgresql-timescaledb.md).

### AI agent integration strategy
- AI agent reads `_index.md` first (always in context)
- Loads specific ADRs only when relevant to current task
- Can search workspace files to find relevant ADRs

## Granularity Guidelines
These guidelines help keep ADR maintenance overhead low while preserving meaningful decision boundaries for a solo project.

Combine when decisions are:
- Tightly coupled – changing one necessitates changing the other
- Same abstraction level – e.g. both infrastructure OR both application-level

===== ADR-016 full =====
# ADR-016: Hardcoded exclusion of pc-switcher's own runtime files from folder sync

Status: Accepted

Date: 2026-07-03

## TL;DR
Folder sync always excludes pc-switcher's own runtime state, install, and logs; these are the only hardcoded excludes — every other exclusion stays user-configurable.

## Implementation Rules
- `FolderSyncJob` MUST always exclude, regardless of user config, the invoking user's:
  - `.local/share/pc-switcher/` (lock file, `sync-history.json`, logs)
  - `.local/share/uv/tools/pcswitcher/` (uv tool install / virtualenv)
  - `.local/bin/pc-switcher` (entry-point shim)
- Excludes MUST be anchored relative to the invoking user's home and only apply when that home is inside the synced folder (no effect when syncing e.g. `/root` as a normal user).
- Hardcoded excludes MUST precede user excludes so a user include rule can never re-expose them.
- These are the ONLY hardcoded excludes. All other exclusions (machine-specific keys, caches, etc.) live in `default-config.yaml` and are user-editable.

## Context
The default folder-sync scope is the real `/home` (and `/root`) mirrored with `rsync --delete`. pc-switcher stores its own runtime state under the user's home: `sync-history.json` (the topology-safety state per ADR-015), the unified lock file, logs, its uv-tool install, and its `~/.local/bin` shim. A naive `/home` mirror would `--delete`-clobber the target's copy of these mid-sync — most damagingly overwriting the target's `sync-history.json` with the source's, corrupting the very state the ADR-015 gates rely on, and overwriting/deleting the running install and the target's own logs.

## Decision
- Exclude pc-switcher's runtime state, install, and logs in code (not merely in the default config), so the protection holds even if a user rewrites their exclude list.
- Keep every other exclusion user-configurable; hardcode nothing else.
- Logs are machine-local: each machine keeps its own; the whole `.local/share/pc-switcher/` directory is excluded rather than enumerating individual files.

## Interaction with user filter rules (#166)

Folder sync now emits user filter rules via two rsync surfaces: a per-folder central `merge <filter_file>` and a tree-wide `dir-merge /.pcswitcher-filter`. The runtime-protection excludes in this ADR remain FIRST of all three groups — ahead of both the central merge and the dir-merge (GLOBAL-FIRST: merge before dir-merge) — so no central `+` and no per-dir `+` rule can ever re-expose pc-switcher's own runtime files.

## Consequences
**Positive**:
- A `/home` mirror can never clobber the target's sync-history, lock, logs, or running install.
- The topology-safety model (ADR-015) stays intact across real-home syncs.
- Minimal, auditable hardcoding; users retain full control over everything else.

**Negative**:
- Only the invoking user's runtime files are protected; other users' pc-switcher state (if any) under `/home` is not specially handled (acceptable under the current single-user model).
- pc-switcher's own logs and history do not propagate between machines (intended: they are machine-local diagnostics/state).

## References
- ADR-013: rsync-over-SSH as user-data transport
- ADR-015: Topology-based sync-safety model
- `src/pcswitcher/jobs/folder_sync.py` (`_RUNTIME_EXCLUDE_RELPATHS`, `_runtime_exclude_filters`, and `_build_rsync_cmd` for the merge/dir-merge emission point)

)

class TestValidatePreflight:
    """validate() enforces sudo rsync availability, acl package, and folder existence."""

    async def test_all_preflight_checks_pass(self) -> None:
        """When all preflight commands succeed, validate() returns no errors."""
        ctx = make_context(config={"folders": [{"path": "/home"}]})
        # source and target run_command already return success by default
        job = FolderSyncJob(ctx)
        errors = await job.validate()
        assert errors == []

    async def test_missing_sudo_rsync_on_target(self) -> None:
        """validate() returns a ValidationError for HOST.TARGET when sudo rsync is unavailable on target."""
        ctx = make_context(config={"folders": [{"path": "/home"}]})
        ctx.target.run_command = AsyncMock(side_effect=fail_when("rsync", "rsync not found"))
        job = FolderSyncJob(ctx)
        errors = await job.validate()
        assert any(e.host == Host.TARGET and "rsync" in e.message.lower() for e in errors)

    async def test_missing_sudo_rsync_on_source(self) -> None:
        """validate() returns a ValidationError for HOST.SOURCE when sudo rsync is unavailable on source."""
        ctx = make_context(config={"folders": [{"path": "/home"}]})
        ctx.source.run_command = AsyncMock(side_effect=fail_when("rsync", "rsync not found"))
        job = FolderSyncJob(ctx)
        errors = await job.validate()
        assert any(e.host == Host.SOURCE and "rsync" in e.message.lower() for e in errors)

    async def test_missing_acl_on_source(self) -> None:
        """validate() returns a ValidationError for HOST.SOURCE when acl package is absent on source."""
        ctx = make_context(config={"folders": [{"path": "/home"}]})
        ctx.source.run_command = AsyncMock(side_effect=fail_when("acl", "no packages found"))
        job = FolderSyncJob(ctx)
        errors = await job.validate()
        assert any(e.host == Host.SOURCE and "acl" in e.message.lower() for e in errors)

    async def test_missing_acl_on_target(self) -> None:
        """validate() returns a ValidationError for HOST.TARGET when acl package is absent on target."""
        ctx = make_context(config={"folders": [{"path": "/home"}]})
        ctx.target.run_command = AsyncMock(side_effect=fail_when("acl", "no packages found"))
        job = FolderSyncJob(ctx)
        errors = await job.validate()
        assert any(e.host == Host.TARGET and "acl" in e.message.lower() for e in errors)

    async def test_missing_source_folder(self) -> None:
        """validate() returns a ValidationError naming the path when an enabled folder is absent on source."""

"""Unit tests for FolderSyncJob.

Tests cover: active-folder selection, validate() preflight (sudo rsync, acl, folder
existence), rsync command construction, and transfer streaming/exit-code handling.

All executor interactions are mocked; no real SSH connections are made.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import shlex
from collections.abc import Callable
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch

import pytest

from pcswitcher.jobs import JobContext
from pcswitcher.jobs.folder_sync import FolderEntry, FolderSyncJob
from pcswitcher.models import CommandResult, FirstSyncScope, Host, LogLevel, ProgressUpdate

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def fail_when(substring: str, stderr: str) -> Callable[..., CommandResult]:
    """Return a run_command side_effect that fails (exit 1) when `substring` is in the command."""

    def _side_effect(cmd: str, **_: object) -> CommandResult:
        if substring in cmd:
            return CommandResult(exit_code=1, stdout="", stderr=stderr)
        return CommandResult(exit_code=0, stdout="", stderr="")

    return _side_effect


def make_context(
    config: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
    dry_run: bool = False,
    target_username: str | None = None,
) -> JobContext:
    """Create a JobContext with mocked source/target executors."""
    source = MagicMock()
    source.run_command = AsyncMock(return_value=CommandResult(exit_code=0, stdout="", stderr=""))
    target = MagicMock()
    target.run_command = AsyncMock(return_value=CommandResult(exit_code=0, stdout="", stderr=""))
    return JobContext(
        config=config if config is not None else {"folders": [{"path": "/home"}]},
        source=source,

class TestValidatePreflight:
    """validate() enforces CPU-arch match, sudo rsync availability, acl package, and folder existence."""

    async def test_all_preflight_checks_pass(self) -> None:
        """When all preflight commands succeed, validate() returns no errors."""
        ctx = make_context(config={"folders": [{"path": "/home"}]})
        # source and target run_command already return success by default
        job = FolderSyncJob(ctx)
        errors = await job.validate()
        assert errors == []

    async def test_arch_mismatch_between_source_and_target(self) -> None:
        """Differing CPU architecture on source vs target is a validation error."""
        ctx = make_context(config={"folders": [{"path": "/home"}]})
        ctx.source.run_command = AsyncMock(side_effect=arch_reports("x86_64"))
        ctx.target.run_command = AsyncMock(side_effect=arch_reports("aarch64"))
        job = FolderSyncJob(ctx)
        errors = await job.validate()
        assert any(e.host == Host.TARGET and "architecture" in e.message.lower() for e in errors)

    async def test_matching_arch_yields_no_error(self) -> None:
        """Identical CPU architecture on both hosts yields no validation error."""
        ctx = make_context(config={"folders": [{"path": "/home"}]})
        ctx.source.run_command = AsyncMock(side_effect=arch_reports("x86_64"))
        ctx.target.run_command = AsyncMock(side_effect=arch_reports("x86_64"))
        job = FolderSyncJob(ctx)
        errors = await job.validate()
        assert errors == []

input.content:
# ADR-017: Mirror pc-switcher's own install; hardcode-exclude only its runtime state

Status: Accepted

Date: 2026-07-20

Supersedes: ADR-016

## TL;DR
Folder sync mirrors pc-switcher's own install (uv tool venv + `~/.local/bin` shim) like any other file; only its runtime *state* directory stays hardcoded-excluded, so the install and the interpreter it depends on always travel together.

## Implementation Rules
- `FolderSyncJob` MUST hardcode-exclude exactly one path, the invoking user's `.local/share/pc-switcher/` (lock file, `sync-history.json`, logs), regardless of user config. This is the ONLY hardcoded exclude.
- The pc-switcher install MUST NOT be excluded: `.local/share/uv/tools/pcswitcher/` (the venv) and `.local/bin/pc-switcher` (the shim) mirror from source like every other uv tool, alongside the uv interpreter tree under `.local/share/uv/python/`.
- The hardcoded exclude MUST anchor relative to the invoking user's home and only apply when that home is inside the synced folder; it MUST precede user filter rules so no `+` rule can re-expose it (unchanged from ADR-016).
- `validate()` MUST abort the sync on a source/target CPU-architecture mismatch (`uname -m`), since the mirrored venv and interpreter are arch-specific binaries.

## Context
ADR-016 hardcode-excluded the pc-switcher install (venv + shim) from the `/home` mirror to keep the "running install" machine-local. But the uv *interpreter* tree (`.local/share/uv/python/`) was not excluded, so it was `--delete`-mirrored to match the source. uv interpreters diverge per machine by patch version, so the frozen venv referenced an interpreter that existed only on the target — and the mirror deleted it, leaving a dangling shebang and a pc-switcher that could not execute on the target (#185). Nothing runs pc-switcher on the target during a sync except the install-on-target step, so the install does not need to be pinned machine-local; it only needs to stay internally consistent with its interpreter.

## Decision
- Hardcode-exclude only `.local/share/pc-switcher/` (runtime state); drop the venv and shim from the exclude set.
- Let the install mirror from source with its interpreter in the same pass, so both come from the source and stay consistent — the same way every other uv tool already syncs.
- Guard the new arch coupling with a `uname -m` source-vs-target check in `validate()` that aborts on mismatch.
- Keep the install-on-target step: it provisions a working pc-switcher on the target before the folder-sync step (relevant to #126), and its output being overwritten by the mirror is harmless.

## Consequences
**Positive**:
- pc-switcher's install and its interpreter can no longer desync; the #185 dangling-interpreter failure is structurally impossible.
- Uniform handling: pc-switcher is no longer a special-cased uv tool.
- The topology-safety state, lock, and logs (ADR-015) stay machine-local, as before.

**Negative**:
- The fleet must be single-architecture; the arch check turns an unsupported heterogeneous fleet into an explicit preflight abort rather than a cryptic exec failure.
- The target's pc-switcher install is overwritten by the source's on every sync (source wins); there is no downgrade guard on the mirrored install.

## References
- ADR-016: Hardcoded exclusion of pc-switcher's own runtime files (superseded by this ADR)
- ADR-015: Topology-based sync-safety model
- ADR-013: rsync-over-SSH as user-data transport
- Issue #185 (root cause and fix); issue #126 (why install-on-target is retained)
- `src/pcswitcher/jobs/folder_sync.py` (`_RUNTIME_EXCLUDE_RELPATHS`, `validate()` arch check)

# ADR-016: Hardcoded exclusion of pc-switcher's own runtime files from folder sync

Status: Accepted

Date: 2026-07-03

## TL;DR
Folder sync always excludes pc-switcher's own runtime state, install, and logs; these are the only hardcoded excludes — every other exclusion stays user-configurable.

## Implementation Rules
- `FolderSyncJob` MUST always exclude, regardless of user config, the invoking user's:
  - `.local/share/pc-switcher/` (lock file, `sync-history.json`, logs)
  - `.local/share/uv/tools/pcswitcher/` (uv tool install / virtualenv)
  - `.local/bin/pc-switcher` (entry-point shim)
- Excludes MUST be anchored relative to the invoking user's home and only apply when that home is inside the synced folder (no effect when syncing e.g. `/root` as a normal user).
- Hardcoded excludes MUST precede user excludes so a user include rule can never re-expose them.
- These are the ONLY hardcoded excludes. All other exclusions (machine-specific keys, caches, etc.) live in `default-config.yaml` and are user-editable.

## Context
The default folder-sync scope is the real `/home` (and `/root`) mirrored with `rsync --delete`. pc-switcher stores its own runtime state under the user's home: `sync-history.json` (the topology-safety state per ADR-015), the unified lock file, logs, its uv-tool install, and its `~/.local/bin` shim. A naive `/home` mirror would `--delete`-clobber the target's copy of these mid-sync — most damagingly overwriting the target's `sync-history.json` with the source's, corrupting the very state the ADR-015 gates rely on, and overwriting/deleting the running install and the target's own logs.

## Decision
- Exclude pc-switcher's runtime state, install, and logs in code (not merely in the default config), so the protection holds even if a user rewrites their exclude list.
- Keep every other exclusion user-configurable; hardcode nothing else.
- Logs are machine-local: each machine keeps its own; the whole `.local/share/pc-switcher/` directory is excluded rather than enumerating individual files.

## Interaction with user filter rules (#166)

Folder sync now emits user filter rules via two rsync surfaces: a per-folder central `merge <filter_file>` and a tree-wide `dir-merge /.pcswitcher-filter`. The runtime-protection excludes in this ADR remain FIRST of all three groups — ahead of both the central merge and the dir-merge (GLOBAL-FIRST: merge before dir-merge) — so no central `+` and no per-dir `+` rule can ever re-expose pc-switcher's own runtime files.

## Consequences
**Positive**:
- A `/home` mirror can never clobber the target's sync-history, lock, logs, or running install.
- The topology-safety model (ADR-015) stays intact across real-home syncs.
- Minimal, auditable hardcoding; users retain full control over everything else.

**Negative**:
- Only the invoking user's runtime files are protected; other users' pc-switcher state (if any) under `/home` is not specially handled (acceptable under the current single-user model).
- pc-switcher's own logs and history do not propagate between machines (intended: they are machine-local diagnostics/state).

## References
- ADR-013: rsync-over-SSH as user-data transport
- ADR-015: Topology-based sync-safety model
- `src/pcswitcher/jobs/folder_sync.py` (`_RUNTIME_EXCLUDE_RELPATHS`, `_runtime_exclude_filters`, and `_build_rsync_cmd` for the merge/dir-merge emission point)

Excluding the venv while `--delete`-mirroring its interpreter tree deleted the in-use interpreter and broke pc-switcher on the target (#185).
Folder sync always excludes pc-switcher's own runtime state, install, and logs; these are the only hardcoded excludes — every other exclusion stays user-configurable.

# Architecture Decisions Summary (Last updated: 2026-07-03)

## Active Decisions

- [ADR-001](adr-001-adr.md): Use Architecture Decision Records (ADR)
- [ADR-002](adr-002-ssh-communication-channel.md): SSH as communication channel between source and target
- [ADR-003](adr-003-implementation-language.md): Python orchestrator with task-specific languages
- [ADR-004](adr-004-dynamic-versioning-github-releases.md): Dynamic package versioning from GitHub Releases
- [ADR-005](adr-005-asyncio-concurrency.md): Use Asyncio for Concurrency
- [ADR-006](adr-006-testing-framework.md): VM-Based Testing Framework
- [ADR-007](adr-007-tdd-implementation.md): Test-Driven Development for Implementation
- [ADR-008](adr-008-ci-pipeline.md): CI Pipeline with Draft-Aware Integration Tests
- [ADR-009](adr-009-ai-readiness-labels.md): AI Readiness Labels for Issue Triage
- [ADR-010](adr-010-logging-infrastructure.md): Standard Library Logging Infrastructure

input.content:
fix(folder_sync): mirror pc-switcher install so it can't desync from its interpreter

The pcswitcher uv tool venv was hardcoded-excluded from the /home mirror while
the uv interpreter tree (.local/share/uv/python/) was --delete-mirrored to match
the source. uv interpreters diverge per machine by patch version, so the frozen
venv referenced an interpreter present only on the target; the mirror deleted it,
leaving a dangling shebang and a pc-switcher that could not execute on the target.

Stop excluding the install (venv + ~/.local/bin shim): it now mirrors from source
alongside its interpreter, like every other uv tool, so both stay consistent. Only
the runtime state (.local/share/pc-switcher) stays machine-local. Add a source/
target `uname -m` preflight that aborts on arch mismatch, since the mirrored
binaries are arch-specific. install_on_target is retained as the #126 hedge.

Supersede ADR-016 with ADR-017.

Fixes #185

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/REDACTED
